A polar bear postman delivering tiny letters across your planner — 15 undated stickers with speech bubbles you get to caption yourself.
Bears running a coffee shop, in sticker form: 15 cozy little scenes with blank bubbles for your own notes. Latte art not guaranteed.
A wee wooden stamp with a grateful cat saying arigato — one press turns any envelope or package into a thank-you note with whiskers.

Teeny lipsticks, polishes and perfume bottles with a foil shimmer — a vanity table for your planner, no cleanup required. Limited run.
Clean little number stamps from a century-old Japanese stamp lab — date your journal pages like a very organized, very charming archivist.
Forty-eight fashion-flavoured flake stickers tucked into a matchbox so cute you'll keep it long after the stickers are gone.
A palm-sized winter note card starring momoro's polar bear — the exact right amount of room for a short, snow-dusted hello.
Mr. Postman the polar bear on a mini note card, ready to carry three sentences of affection to someone's mailbox or lunchbox.
A mini note card from Bear's Coffee Shop — slip one into a gift or lunch bag and serve someone a small warm moment.
A bilingual 'invoice enclosed' stamp that makes even billing people feel gentle — the small-shop owner's most charming admin tool.
Botanicals carved into erasers, then pressed onto tape: HUTTE's hand-stamped greenery makes journal edges look like pressed-flower pages.
Sunny Sunday's collage-print washi rolls out ten metres of artful scraps — instant depth for spreads when you can't be bothered to collage.
Wind instruments parading down a washi roll — Sunny Sunday's quirkiest tape yet, for journal pages that deserve a tiny brass section.
A flock of colourful little birds from Kyoto's Subikiawa, ready to perch along your planner margins and envelope seams.
Wrapped candies marching down seven metres of mt washi — sweeten a planner page with zero calories and maximum nostalgia.
The Shibu set: five muted, moody Mildliners with dual tips — highlight your notes in colours that whisper instead of shout.
Nagomi means calm, and these five gentle Mildliner shades live up to it — dual-tip highlighting that never bleeds through your planner.
Five softened neons with thick-and-thin dual tips — all the highlighter energy, none of the retina burn. Planner people, assemble.
A summer fruit stand on a sticker sheet, with foil accents that glint when the light hits — peak juicy energy for planner pages.
Yohaku's spring palette on tape: faded paper fragments in bloom-soft hues, for journal spreads that feel like found ephemera.
A quiet collage of old-paper fragments running the length of the roll — Yohaku's signature blank-space beauty for slow journaling days.
Soft geometric shapes scattered like paper confetti — Yohaku's understated way of making a page feel gently composed.
A wide grid tape with the worn softness of an old ledger page — Yohaku turns graph paper into something you want to frame.
Faded dots on paper-textured tape — the polka pattern for people who like their whimsy quiet and their journals serene.
Gentle stripes with the softness of sun-faded stationery — Yohaku's take on the humble stripe, made for calm collage layers.
A tiny forest wrapped around a roll — Classiky's woodland washi brings a walk among the trees to your envelopes and journal edges.
The gel pen that launched a thousand planner spreads: Sarasa Clip glides smooth, clips anywhere, and comes in every colour you need.
Forest or butterflies, take your pick — two Classiky washi designs that flutter storybook charm onto anything paper-shaped.
Sarasa's beloved gel ink in moody vintage shades — sepia, dusty blues and muted greens that make grocery lists look like poetry drafts.
A flexible brush tip that swings from hairline to bold — the gateway pen for anyone flirting with modern calligraphy.
Collage-print address labels that dress up parcels and letters in vintage paper scraps — snail mail never looked so intentional.
A porcelain-handled 'receipt included' stamp — paperwork, but make it heirloom. Once it's gone, it's gone for good.
Six collage stickers edged like postage stamps — instant mail-art credibility for planners, envelopes and gift tags.
Liang Feng's painterly birds glide across ten metres of washi — a limited-run flock worth hoarding for your best spreads.
Die-cut crimson blooms from Chamil Garden's hand-carved stamps — petals that peel off the roll ready to scatter across a page.
